compiled_state.title: Sprint Phase 6 validation dispatched general-purpose agent instead of dso:completion-verifier
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

During sprint f997-ed78 Phase 6 validation (Step 0.75 and the remediation re-validation), the orchestrator dispatched a general-purpose sub-agent with a custom prompt instead of the dedicated `dso:completion-verifier` named agent.

## Rationalization Applied (that should not have been)

The orchestrator inlined the verification logic in the sub-agent prompt rather than invoking `subagent_type: dso:completion-verifier`. The implicit rationalization was:
- The sub-agent received explicit success criteria and a structured scoring rubric inline
- The custom prompt was 'equivalent' to what the verifier would do
- Dispatching a named agent vs. a general-purpose agent with an equivalent prompt produces the same result

CLAUDE.md rule #24 explicitly prohibits this: 'Never skip dso:completion-verifier dispatch or substitute inline verification — the verifier exists because the orchestrator is biased toward confirming its own work. Inline verification is NOT a substitute.'

## Why This Is a Real Bug

1. The orchestrator is biased toward confirming its own work — exactly the failure mode rule #24 prevents
2. The general-purpose agent has no system-prompt calibration for verification tasks (no scoring rubric, no severity thresholds)
3. The sub-agent scored the first validation at 3/5 and found real gaps — so it worked, but this was luck not design
4. Future orchestrators will repeat this rationalization unless the SKILL.md makes the dispatch mandatory more explicitly

## Fix

Sprint SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 0.75 should have explicit dispatch instructions using `subagent_type: dso:completion-verifier` with no inline substitution path. The orchestrator must not be able to rationalize away the named agent by providing an 'equivalent' custom prompt.
